text.title: OKR Setting Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding OKR Setting. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: ≤3 objectives per quarter; more = none get done.
rule.rationale: ≤3 objectives per quarter; more = none get done.

rule.statement: ≥2 of 3 KRs per objective are leading indicators, not lagging.
rule.rationale: ≥2 of 3 KRs per objective are leading indicators, not lagging.

rule.statement: Each KR carries a confidence band (e.g. 50/70/90%).
rule.rationale: Each KR carries a confidence band (e.g. 50/70/90%).

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-okr-setting.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
